notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: This page seems to have lost its way.
notFound.reassurance: No worries, we'll get you back on track.

time.title: Quality time, restored
time.description: Fewer meltdowns, less mental load. 2 minutes each evening is enough to spot what calms your child and set up a quieter day.

rgpd.title: GDPR · data hosted in the EU
rgpd.description: Your health data stays in Europe, encrypted, and belongs to you. Full GDPR export anytime.

parents.title: Built by a parent, not a clinic
parents.description: Tokō is built solo by a concerned parent — not a lab or a medical team. Health decisions stay between you and your healthcare professional.

t1.quote: Finally a tool that doesn't guilt-trip me. I take 2 minutes each evening, and when we see the specialist, she has everything right there.
t1.author: Sophie, mother of Léo (8)

t2.quote: The full-screen crisis list is a game-changer. My son knows where to look when he feels it rising.
t2.author: Karim, father of Ilyes (6)

t3.quote: The step-by-step Barkley program gave me structure. I stopped flying blind.
t3.author: Elise, mother of Sacha (10)

resourcesTeaser.title: Understand and support your child
resourcesTeaser.description: Clear guides on everyday parenting — routines, sleep, emotions — with an ADHD section for families who need it. Drawn from public scientific publications, they don't replace a healthcare professional's advice.

hero.description: Tokō helps parents of children with ADHD bring calm back to daily life. Spot what soothes your child, reduce meltdowns, and free up quality time — to rebuild the bond and feel at home in family life again.
hero.noCard: No credit card. 2 minutes to get started.

features.title: Your child's daily life, all in one place
features.subtitle: Built by a parent, for parents — with extra support for families dealing with ADHD.
features.andMore: Also included: daily journal, symptom tracking, medication tracking, dashboard, evening reminders, resources & news.

carnet.title: ADHD consultation logbook
carnet.description: Ready for your next appointment: trends, journal, medication, and your questions for the doctor. Print or send in one click. Free for 30-day windows.

symptoms.title: Daily tracking
symptoms.description: Rate your child's mood, sleep, focus and restlessness each day, and keep track of the routines that work.

journal.title: Observation journal
journal.description: Record notable daily events with thematic tags (school, crisis, victory…).

medications.title: Medication tracking
medications.description: Manage your child's treatments, track daily intake and note any side effects.

crisis.title: Calm-down list
crisis.description: Build a list of calming activities with your child, viewable in full-screen mode when tension rises — meltdowns, tantrums, tough moments.

rewards.title: Reward chart
rewards.description: Track target behaviors week after week and unlock rewards with earned stars.

barkley.title: Barkley program · ADHD option
barkley.description: For families dealing with ADHD: the 10 steps of Dr. Barkley's parent training program (PEHP), with educational content and a quiz at each step. Enable it if you need it.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.description: Visualize trends over week, month, or quarter, and identify the patterns that matter.

news.title: News & resources
news.description: A feed of articles about parenting, daily routines and ADHD, drawn from public sources and scientific publications.

reminders.title: Reminders & evening check
reminders.description: A 2-minute ritual every evening, with Web Push notifications to keep track of daily life — even on the busiest days.

pricing.title: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing.subtitle: Start free and change daily life at home. The Family plan unlocks long-term tracking, multi-child support and everything that helps bring lasting calm to family life.
pricing.annualEquivalent: ≈ €60/year — a few coffees a month to get your time back
pricing.comparisonFooter: Cancel anytime · Secure Stripe payment · Invoice sent by email

family.name: Family
family.description: For titration, siblings and long-term tracking
family.feature3: Send the logbook to the doctor by email

footer.tagline: Tokō — Fewer meltdowns, more time together

login.tagline: The app that helps parents of children with ADHD calm daily life — and find more time together.
login.name: Name

forgotPassword.title: Forgot password
forgotPassword.intro: Enter your email and we'll send you a link to choose a new password.
forgotPassword.confirmation: If an account exists for this address, an email is on its way. Check your inbox (and spam folder).

resetPassword.title: New password
resetPassword.success: Done. Sign in with your new password.
resetPassword.errorInvalid: Link expired or invalid. Request a new one.

contact.title: Contact
contact.contactUsBody: For any question, suggestion, or request about Tokō, feel free to contact us by email.
contact.rightsBody: In accordance with the GDPR, you can exercise your rights of access, rectification, erasure, and portability of your data by writing to the address above. We commit to responding within 30 days.
contact.reportBody: If you encounter a bug or technical issue, please report it by email with the most precise steps to reproduce.

lock.title: Screen locked
lock.description: Tokō hides your dashboard to protect your child's privacy. Tap continue to resume.

theme.label: Appearance
theme.description: Choose the interface theme. System mode follows your device preferences.
theme.darkDescription: Dark background, easier on the eyes at night.

legal.title: Legal notice
legal.editorBody: Tokō is a web application published on a personal basis.
legal.hostingBody: This site is hosted by cloud hosting services. Data is stored on secure servers.
legal.ipBody: All content on this site (text, images, graphics, logo, icons) is the exclusive property of the publisher, unless otherwise stated. Any reproduction, representation, modification, or adaptation of all or part of the site is forbidden without prior authorization.
legal.personalDataBody1: Data collected through this application is used only within the scope of the tracking provided by Tokō. No personal data is sold or transmitted to third parties for commercial purposes.
legal.personalDataBody2: In accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), you have a right of access, rectification, erasure, and portability of your data. To exercise these rights, please contact us.
legal.cookiesBody: This site uses cookies strictly necessary for the operation of the application (authentication, session). No advertising tracking cookies are used.
legal.liabilityBody: Tokō is a tracking aid tool and in no way constitutes medical advice. Information provided by the application does not replace a consultation with a healthcare professional. The publisher cannot be held responsible for any use made of the information on this site.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.introBody: Tokō is committed to protecting the privacy of its users. This privacy policy describes the data we collect, how we use it, and the measures we take to protect it.
privacy.collectedAccountBody: email address and name, when you create your account.
privacy.collectedTrackingBody: symptoms and journal notes you enter in the application.
privacy.collectedTechnicalBody: session cookies required for the application to work.
privacy.usageIntro: Your data is used exclusively to:
privacy.usage1: Provide the tracking features of the application.
privacy.usage2: Generate reports and statistics visible only to you.
privacy.usage3: Improve the operation and reliability of the service.
privacy.sharingBody: We do not sell, rent, or share your personal data with third parties for commercial purposes. Your health tracking data remains strictly confidential.
privacy.securityBody: We implement technical and organizational security measures to protect your data against unauthorized access, modification, disclosure, or destruction. Connections are encrypted and passwords are hashed.
privacy.rightsIntro: In accordance with the GDPR, you have the following rights:
privacy.rightAccessBody: obtain a copy of your personal data.
privacy.rightDeleteBody: request the deletion of your data.
privacy.rightPortabilityBody: receive your data in a structured, readable format.
privacy.rightObjectBody: object to the processing of your data.
privacy.rightsContact: To exercise these rights, please contact us via the contact page.
privacy.retentionBody: Your data is retained as long as your account is active. If you delete your account, all your personal data will be removed from our servers.
privacy.updatesBody: This privacy policy may be updated from time to time. Any changes will be published on this page.

dashboard.welcomeSubtitle: Start by adding your child to begin tracking.
dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Daily tracking overview

tips.selfCompassion: You are doing your best with a situation that few parents truly understand. Be as gentle with yourself as you are with your child.
tips.smallWins: Today, note one small win — yours or your child's. ADHD progress is measured in weeks, not hours.
tips.coRegulation: Before asking your child to calm down, lower your own voice by a notch. Their brain mirrors yours, not the other way around.
tips.predictableRoutine: A routine repeated at the same time every day frees up mental load — yours and theirs.
tips.namingEmotions: Naming what they feel ("you're frustrated") lowers the intensity. You don't need to fix it, just name it.
tips.shortInstructions: One instruction at a time, eye contact. Long sentences get lost in an ADHD brain.
tips.celebrate: Celebrate effort, not outcome. "You tried even when it was hard" is more powerful than "you succeeded."
tips.yourSleep: Your sleep matters as much as theirs. Go to bed 30 minutes earlier tonight if you can — it's the foundation of your patience tomorrow.
tips.askForHelp: Asking for help isn't failure, it's strategy. Co-parent, grandparent, friend: who can you call today?
tips.transitionWarning: Before any transition (leaving, mealtime, bedtime), give a 5-minute warning. The ADHD brain needs that buffer.
tips.physicalRelease: When tension rises, step outside with them for 10 minutes. Movement releases what words can't.

dashboard.inactivityBody: Remember to log today's symptoms to keep your streak.

account.title: My account
account.subtitle: Manage your personal data and GDPR rights

account.pauseQuotaExceeded: Annual pause quota reached. You'll be able to pause again starting January 1st.
account.pauseTrialingError: Pause is available once your trial has ended.
account.pauseCancelPendingError: Cancellation is already scheduled. Reactivate your subscription before pausing it.
account.pauseGenericError: Couldn't pause the subscription right now.
account.resumeError: Couldn't resume the subscription right now. Please try again in a moment.
account.cancelScheduledHint: Your subscription will end on {{date}}. You can reactivate it any time before that date.
account.checkoutError: Couldn't start checkout right now. Please try again in a moment.
account.portalError: Couldn't open the management portal. Please try again in a moment.
account.exportDataDescription: Download a copy of all your personal data in JSON format (GDPR Art. 20 — Right to portability)
account.deleteAccountDescription: Permanently deletes your account and all associated data (GDPR Art. 17 — Right to erasure). This action is irreversible.
account.confirmDeletionDescription: This action is irreversible. All your data will be permanently deleted: profile, children, symptoms, medications, journal, and subscription.
account.medicalReportDescription: PDF summary to bring to appointments (child psychiatrist, pediatrician, speech therapist)

welcome.title: Welcome to Tokō
welcome.body: Tokō supports you day to day in caring for your ADHD child. Let's take a quick tour of the essential features together.

dashboard.title: Your dashboard
dashboard.body: Check mood, consistency, weekly stars and a fresh tip every day. Everything starts here.

tracking.title: Symptoms & journal
tracking.body: Log symptoms in seconds and keep a free-form journal. Trends appear automatically after a few days of tracking.

routines.title: Routines & rewards
routines.body: Build calming routines and a star reward chart to encourage positive behaviours, one star at a time.

care.title: Crisis support
care.body: Crisis list, medications, care pathway and an SOS button — everything you need to stay grounded, even on tough days.

discover.title: A first foothold
discover.body: Three short reads and one concrete protocol to start with. Pick the one that matches your situation — the rest stay one click away in the menu.

ready.title: You're all set
ready.body: Start by adding your child and a first entry. You can relaunch this tour anytime from the user menu.

crisis.title: Understanding an ADHD crisis
crisis.subtitle: The full guide — best read before the next one hits.

diagnosis.title: After the diagnosis
diagnosis.subtitle: Care pathway — who to consult and when.

bedtime.title: Bedtime without yelling
bedtime.subtitle: A ready-to-use protocol you can try tonight.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: Emails sent according to your timezone.
notifications.morningReminderBody: An email to log how the morning went if nothing has been logged.
notifications.eveningReminderBody: An email to log how the day went if nothing has been written in the journal.
notifications.coParentActivityBody: Push when your co-parent logs a symptom, journal entry, or medication.

share.dialogTitle: Build a bridge with a loved one
share.dialogDescription: You're not alone in carrying this. Send a simple page, written to be read without prior knowledge, so a loved one can understand what your child is going through.
share.privacyHint: No data about your child is included.

journal.title: Journal
journal.subtitle: Daily notes and observations
journal.selectChild: Select a child to view their journal.
journal.emptyState: Your journal is empty. Start by writing your first observations.
journal.deleteBodyPrefix: This action is irreversible. The entry from
journal.notesPlaceholder: A victory, a struggle, a strategy that helped…

symptoms.title: Symptoms
symptoms.subtitle: Daily symptom tracking
symptoms.selectChild: Select a child to view their symptoms.
symptoms.emptyState: No symptom entries yet. Start by adding one.
symptoms.conflictBanner: An entry already exists for this date. Your changes will replace it.
symptoms.smartDefaults: Values pre-filled from the last entry.
symptoms.deleteBody: This action is irreversible. The data for this entry will be permanently deleted.

rewards.title: Rewards
rewards.subtitle: Reward chart to unlock with stars
rewards.selectChild: Select a child to access rewards.
rewards.emptyBody: Add motivating rewards that your child can unlock by earning stars.
rewards.starsNeededHelp: Cost in stars to unlock this reward.
rewards.reachableNow: Already reachable with the current balance.
rewards.reachable1Day: Reachable in about 1 day at the current pace.
rewards.reachableDays: Reachable in about {{days}} days at the current pace.
rewards.reachableStart: Start checking behaviors to estimate timing.

timer.title: Visual timer
timer.subtitle: A dial that visibly empties helps your child — and you — feel how much time is left for a task: homework, brushing teeth, gaming, transitions.
timer.speedUpHint: For little challenges: tidying up, getting dressed, brushing teeth.

companion.hatched: Yay, a new companion has arrived!
companion.tryAgain: Well done for trying. We'll give it another go next time.

achievements.title: My parent badges
achievements.subtitle: Every action counts. Here are the milestones you've crossed with Tokō — for you, not your child. No one else celebrates your consistency.
achievements.disclaimer: Badges update live based on the active child's data. No leaderboard, no pressure — just a mirror of what you've built.

first_child.title: First step
first_child.description: You added your first child. Tracking starts here.

first_journal.title: First observation
first_journal.description: First journal entry. Your notes are gold for the specialist.

first_strength.title: Kind eye
first_strength.description: You logged a strength of your child. Keep going — this is what rebalances everything.

five_strengths.title: Pride catalogue
five_strengths.description: 5 strengths recorded. You're seeing your child from a different angle.

first_crisis_list.title: Calm-down kit
first_crisis_list.description: First soothing activity listed. You have a Plan B when tension rises.

first_routine.title: Routine on board
first_routine.description: First routine created. Daily life takes shape.

streak_7.title: A full week
streak_7.description: 7 consecutive days of tracking. Consistency pays off.

streak_30.title: A month standing
streak_30.description: 30 consecutive days of tracking. You stuck with it — that's rare.

first_care_step_done.title: First step crossed
first_care_step_done.description: One care-pathway step ticked off. Real progress, keep going.

screening_phase_done.title: Screening complete
screening_phase_done.description: All screening-phase steps done. Diagnosis is in sight.

first_doc_vault.title: Archives in order
first_doc_vault.description: First document in the vault. Never again panic the night before an appointment.

explorer.title: Explorer
explorer.description: You've used 5 different features. Tokō has become your toolbox.

adminVault.title: Document vault
adminVault.subtitle: All your child's reports, letters and dossiers in one place. Encrypted, accessible only to you, ready for the next MDPH or medical appointment.
adminVault.selectChild: Select a child to see their documents.
adminVault.emptyBody: Speech-therapy report, neuropsych assessment, prescription, MDPH dossier… Store the PDFs and photos here so you never search for them the night before an appointment.
adminVault.noMatch: No documents in this category yet.
adminVault.deleteBodySuffix: ” will be permanently removed from the vault.

fields.fileHint: PDF, JPEG or PNG — 10 MB max.
fields.title: Title

errors.mime: Unsupported format. Use a PDF, JPEG or PNG.

carePathway.title: ADHD care pathway
carePathway.subtitle: The French medical and administrative maze, step by step. Tick off what's done, keep track of what's next — without drowning. (Content is France-specific.)
carePathway.selectChild: Select a child to see their pathway.
carePathway.disclaimer: This pathway is indicative. The order and required steps vary per child and region — your doctor remains your best guide.

screening.title: 1. Screening
screening.description: Before chasing ADHD, rule out what can mimic it: sleep, hearing, vision, language, motor skills.

diagnosis.title: 2. Diagnosis
diagnosis.description: Formal medical diagnosis goes through a qualified doctor, after a neuropsych assessment.

support.title: 3. Support & follow-up
support.description: MDPH paperwork, school accommodations, multidisciplinary follow-up — what makes day-to-day livable.

school_signal.title: School-based screening
school_signal.description: Talk to the teacher or school doctor. Ask whether they observe attention, restlessness or behaviour issues compared to the age group.

gp_consultation.title: GP / paediatrician consultation
gp_consultation.description: First medical step: your doctor listens, examines your child, and refers for the relevant assessments. Ask for a long, dedicated visit.

ent_audition.title: ENT & hearing test
ent_audition.description: Imperfect hearing can mimic an attention disorder. ENT visit with audiogram — often requested by the paediatrician before any other workup.

ophtalmo_vision.title: Eye exam
ophtalmo_vision.description: Check vision with an orthoptist or ophthalmologist: an uncorrected vision issue can look like inattention in class.

sleep_study.title: Sleep study (if suspected)
sleep_study.description: Apnoea, restless sleep, loud snoring? A paediatric polysomnography rules out an organic cause before discussing ADHD.

speech_therapy_assessment.title: Speech-language assessment
speech_therapy_assessment.description: Rules out oral and written language disorders (dyslexia, dysorthography) which can co-occur with or be mistaken for ADHD.

psychomotor_assessment.title: Psychomotor assessment
psychomotor_assessment.description: Evaluates gross and fine motor skills, coordination and attention. Available via PCO platform or in private practice — often a prerequisite to neuropsych.

neuropsy_assessment.title: Neuropsychological assessment
neuropsy_assessment.description: Heart of the diagnosis: WISC-V, NEPSY, BRIEF — measures attention, executive function, working memory. 2 to 4 sessions. Long waitlists, book early.

specialist_consultation.title: Neuropaediatrician / child psychiatrist
specialist_consultation.description: The qualified doctor confirms the medical diagnosis after reviewing the assessments. Bring all prior reports. CMP/CRA waits are often ≥ 6 months — private practice is faster but costly.

diagnosis_announcement.title: Diagnosis disclosure
diagnosis_announcement.description: Crucial moment: ask all your questions, request a written report, plan follow-up. Prepare your questions in advance — to stay grounded in the emotion of the moment.

second_opinion.title: Second opinion (if needed)
second_opinion.description: If the diagnosis leaves you uncertain or recommendations feel incomplete, you have every right to consult another specialist. It's normal and useful.

mdph_application.title: MDPH application
mdph_application.description: The Maison Départementale des Personnes Handicapées unlocks support (AEEH, PCH, AVS, PPS). Long file to compile, 4 to 6 months processing. Get help from a local ADHD association.

aeeh_request.title: AEEH request
aeeh_request.description: Disabled Child Education Allowance: monthly financial support (≈ €150/month base, with complements based on family-life impact). Filed inside the MDPH application.

pch_request.title: PCH (if eligible)
pch_request.description: Disability Compensation Benefit: covers extra costs (paramedical follow-up, accommodations) when AEEH doesn't suffice. Can be combined with AEEH complement levels 3, 4, 5 or 6.

school_pap_pps.title: PAP or PPS at school
school_pap_pps.description: Personalised Support Plan (PAP) for moderate needs, or Personalised Schooling Plan (PPS) via MDPH for heavier accommodations (AVS aide, ULIS class). Request to the school principal.

occupational_therapy.title: Multidisciplinary follow-up
occupational_therapy.description: As needed: occupational therapist (handwriting, organisation), psychomotor therapist, neuropsych (executive function rehabilitation), psychologist. Often private practice, sometimes covered by PCO.

ongoing_followup.title: Ongoing medical follow-up
ongoing_followup.description: Yearly review with the neuropaediatrician/child psychiatrist, possible medication adjustment, growth tracking. ADHD isn't static — care evolves with age.

strengths.title: Strengths
strengths.subtitle: Your child's talents, achievements, qualities and milestones — to balance the lens and feed the whole family's self-esteem.
strengths.selectChild: Select a child to see their strengths.
strengths.emptyTitle: What if there's more than the struggles?
strengths.emptyBody: Note a talent, an achievement, a quality or a milestone here. One entry a week is enough to shift how you see your child — and how they see themselves.
strengths.noMatch: No strengths in this category yet.

fields.title: Title
fields.titlePlaceholder: e.g. Tied their shoes by themselves
fields.descriptionPlaceholder: Context, the pride felt, what made this possible…

sos.title: Take a breath. You're in the right place.
sos.subtitle: Pick one technique to bring things down. No judgement — you're already carrying a lot.

breathing.title: Guided breathing
breathing.description: Calm your nervous system in 1 minute before turning back to your child.

sensory.title: Sensory reset
sensory.description: Lower the input so your child can come back down.

diversion.title: Redirect
diversion.description: A few ideas to shift attention to something soothing.

breathing.title: Breathe with me
breathing.intro: Follow the circle. Breathe in as it grows, breathe out as it shrinks. Stay with it for 1 to 2 minutes.

sensory.title: Lower the input
sensory.intro: A meltdown is often overload. Reduce what's coming into their brain first.
steps: Dim the lights or move to a quiet room.
Turn off music, TV and notifications.
Talk less, lower, slower — your words are input too.
Offer a comfort item: stuffed toy, weighted blanket, noise-cancelling headphones.

diversion.title: Shift their focus
diversion.intro: Once the peak has passed, offer one simple, concrete option. No negotiation — just an invitation.
diversion.introCustom: Here are the activities you've listed together with your child. Offer one, no bargaining — just an invitation.

ideas.label: Share a big glass of water

crisis.title: Crisis list
crisis.subtitle: The things that help your child when they're not okay
crisis.subtitleWithName: The things that help {{name}} when they're not okay
crisis.selectChild: Select a child to view their crisis list.
crisis.emptyBody: Build this list with your child: what helps them when things aren't okay?
crisis.deleteBody: "{{label}}" will be removed from the crisis list. This action is irreversible.
crisis.ratingBody: A quick answer helps us learn which activities really work.

routines.title: Routines
routines.subtitle: Small steps that make the day more predictable.
routines.selectChild: Select a child to view their routines.
routines.emptyBody: Break the day into small steps: morning, homework, bedtime… Tick each step with your child to make daily life easier to follow.
routines.daysHint: Leave empty for a daily routine.
routines.deleteBody: "{{name}}" and all its steps will be removed. This action is irreversible.
routines.stepsHint: Tiny steps, big effect. Aim for 3 to 6 short steps per routine.

barkleyInfo.title: Why tiny steps?
barkleyInfo.intro: Dr. Russell Barkley, a leading authority on ADHD, has shown that breaking tasks into micro-steps compensates for the executive function deficit specific to ADHD.
barkleyInfo.benefitsTitle: What it changes for the child
barkleyInfo.benefit1: Less mental overload, so less opposition.
barkleyInfo.benefit2: Each checked box becomes a visible win that boosts self-esteem.
barkleyInfo.benefit3: The routine becomes predictable: less anxiety, fewer meltdowns.
barkleyInfo.rangeBody: Beyond that, working memory gets overwhelmed. Below it, the routine no longer provides enough structure.
barkleyInfo.exampleBody: Instead of "Tidy your room," try: 1) Clothes in the basket · 2) Toys in the chest · 3) Books on the shelf.

templates.modalIntro: Pick a ready-made routine. You can edit everything afterwards.
templates.startHereIntro: Don't feel like starting from scratch? Routines tested by other parents.
templates.patienceBody: A routine takes 3 to 6 weeks to settle in. The first days will feel chaotic — that's expected. Try 14 days before changing it.

barkley.title: Barkley program
barkley.subtitle: Parent training program (PEHP)
barkley.selectChild: Select a child to access the Barkley program.
barkley.saveErrorBody: There was a save error, try again below.
barkley.allCorrect: All answers are correct. Well done!

formation.disclaimer: This psychoeducational program is inspired by Barkley's work on parenting skills. It does not replace professional support from a psychologist or psychiatrist.
formation.pehpTitle: Find a parent training group near you
formation.pehpDesc: To go further, look for a Parent Training Program led by a professional. Ask your local mental health center, ADHD associations, or your child's psychiatrist.
formation.quizReadyDesc: Answer the {{count}} questions to validate your understanding of this step.
formation.quizCompletedDesc: You have validated the quiz for this step. You can re-read the content at any time.

child.randomNicknameTooltip: Generate a random nickname to protect your child's privacy
child.secondChildUpsellTitle: Up to 3 profiles with the Family plan
child.secondChildUpsellBody: The free plan covers one child profile. Upgrade to Family to track up to 3 children, with full history and detailed reports.
child.secondChildUpsellNoCard: No credit card · Cancel in one click
child.deleteBody: This action is irreversible. All data for <1>{{name}}</1> will be permanently deleted: symptoms, journal, crisis list, behaviors, and Barkley rewards.

childAccess.descriptionOwner: Invite another adult in the household to view and update this child's logbook. Co-parents share your plan — no second subscription needed.
childAccess.descriptionCoParent: You access this child as a co-parent. The owner manages invitations and billing.
childAccess.attestationLabel: I hold parental authority over this child and authorise sharing their health logbook with the invited person.
childAccess.attestationRequired: You must confirm you hold parental authority.
childAccess.consentNotice: By accepting, I give explicit consent to process the child's health data (GDPR Art. 9). I can withdraw access at any time from my account.
childAccess.consentNoticeShort: Data shared: symptoms, journal, medications, Barkley programme, appointments.
childAccess.controllerNotice: Data controller: Tokō. Retention: for the lifetime of the account. Email sent via Resend (EU/US). You can exercise your rights or contact your DPA at any time.
childAccess.noCoParentYet: No one else has access yet. You manage this child's logbook on your own.

familyShare.cardDescription: Share all your children with another parent in one step. They'll see and update their logbooks like you, on the same subscription.
familyShare.emptyDescription: No co-parent has access to your children yet.
familyShare.noOwnedChildren: Add a child first before sharing.
familyShare.revokeConfirmDescription: {{name}} will lose access to all your children. You can invite them again later.
familyShare.dialogTitle: Who do you want to share with?
familyShare.dialogStep1Description: Enter the co-parent's email. You'll choose which children to share next.
familyShare.dialogStep2Description: All your children are selected by default. Uncheck any you don't want to share.
familyShare.attestationLabel: I hold parental authority over the shared children and authorise sharing their health logbook.
familyShare.advancedTitle: Special case: share a single child
familyShare.advancedDescription: Useful for split custody or per-child parental authority. Select a child from the dashboard first.

invitePage.title: You're invited to co-parent
invitePage.subtitle: Tokō · shared ADHD logbook
invitePage.expiredHelp: Ask the parent who sent it to you for a new invitation.
invitePage.intro: {{inviter}} would like to share the logbook for {{child}} with you. You'll be able to view and update symptoms, journal, medications, the Barkley programme, and appointments — with the same access as {{inviter}}.
invitePage.introBulk_one: {{inviter}} would like to share {{count}} child's logbook with you. You'll be able to view and update symptoms, journal, medications, the Barkley programme, and appointments — with the same access as {{inviter}}.
invitePage.introBulk_other: {{inviter}} would like to share {{count}} children's logbooks with you. You'll be able to view and update symptoms, journal, medications, the Barkley programme, and appointments — with the same access as {{inviter}}.

tips.dashboard-same-time-ritual: Check Tokō at the same time each evening to anchor tracking as a family ritual.
tips.dashboard-celebrate-streak: Show the streak to your child: seeing their progress boosts self-esteem.
tips.dashboard-mood-conversation: If mood drops 2 days in a row, open a calm conversation without trying to fix.
tips.symptoms-observe-not-judge: Note what you observe today, without comparing to yesterday or feeling guilty.
tips.symptoms-context-matters: Always specify the context: sleep, school, or screens change everything in the analysis.
tips.symptoms-with-child-from-8: From age 8, fill it out with your child to develop their self-awareness.
tips.journal-one-positive-daily: Note at least one positive thing each day, however tiny, to rebalance your view.
tips.journal-describe-triggers: Describe what precedes a crisis: you'll identify your triggers in a few weeks.
tips.journal-short-is-enough: Two sentences are enough: regularity matters more than note length.
tips.crisis-build-when-calm: Build the list with your child when they're calm, never during a crisis.
tips.crisis-test-before-crisis: Test each activity outside of crisis to check it truly soothes them.
tips.crisis-mix-sensory-types: Mix senses: a body activity, a sensory one, a cuddly one, a creative one.
tips.crisis-offer-not-impose: Offer without imposing: let your child choose their activity, even if they refuse at first.
tips.crisis-stay-close: Stay nearby in silence: your presence regulates more than your words during the storm.
tips.rewards-immediate-star: Give the star within 30 seconds of the action: immediacy is essential for ADHD.
tips.rewards-never-remove: Never remove a star already earned: use privilege removal separately.
tips.rewards-start-small: Start with 2 to 3 behaviors max to avoid discouraging your child.
tips.barkley-one-step-per-week: Spend at least a week on each step before moving to the next.
tips.barkley-special-time-first: Install step 2's special moments before any rules: connection precedes discipline.

barkleySteps.title: Why does my child behave this way?
barkleySteps.description: Understand the nature of ADHD and oppositional defiant disorder (ODD). This first step lays the foundation by explaining your child's neurobiological characteristics and the reasons behind their behaviors.

barkleySteps.title: Give positive attention to your child
barkleySteps.description: Learn to create quality "special moments" with your child to rebuild the family bond. Valuing positive behaviors rather than focusing on the negative helps restore calm communication.

barkleySteps.title: Increase compliance: effective commands
barkleySteps.description: Learn to formulate clear, structured requests in three steps. This step teaches how to give instructions your child can understand and follow, paired with positive recognition of their cooperation.
barkleySteps.linkLabel: Ideereka — The 10 steps of the Barkley program

barkleySteps.title: Teach your child not to interrupt your activities
barkleySteps.description: Help your child develop autonomy and the ability to wait. This step aims for everyone to find their place in the family, by teaching the child to respect times when you're busy.

barkleySteps.title: Set up a token system at home
barkleySteps.description: Create a system of points, stickers, or tokens to reward expected behaviors. This positive reinforcement transforms daily interactions and motivates your child to adopt good behaviors in a playful way.
barkleySteps.linkLabel: Ideereka — The 10 steps of the Barkley program

barkleySteps.title: Use privilege removal
barkleySteps.description: Learn to use response cost fairly and consistently. When the child doesn't follow instructions, removing points or privileges is a logical, proportionate consequence without traditional punishment.

barkleySteps.title: Time-out
barkleySteps.description: Replace punishment with structured calm moments. Time-out lets the child be alone with themselves to cool down emotionally before resuming the conversation calmly.
barkleySteps.linkLabel: Ideereka — The 10 steps of the Barkley program

barkleySteps.title: Manage behaviors outside the home
barkleySteps.description: Generalize learned techniques outside: shops, restaurants, family outings. This step also includes collaborating with school and setting up a daily behavior report.

barkleySteps.title: Manage future behavior problems
barkleySteps.description: Gain autonomy to anticipate and prevent the resurgence of maladaptive behaviors. You learn to identify risky situations and prepare adapted strategies before a problem arises.
barkleySteps.linkLabel: Ideereka — The 10 steps of the Barkley program

barkleySteps.title: Review and maintaining progress
barkleySteps.description: Review the progress made and consolidate the skills acquired throughout the program. This final step helps you maintain positive changes over time and continue independently.

news.title: Knowledge base
news.subtitle: Everything about ADHD: guides, tips and resources to better understand and support your child daily.
news.emptyHint: Check back soon for the latest updates.

install.title: Install Tokō on your device
install.body: Open Tokō in one tap, even offline. Add the app to your home screen — no app store needed.
install.iosBody: On iPhone, add Tokō to your home screen to use it like a native app.

pwaUpdate.title: A new version is available
pwaUpdate.body: Reload Tokō to get the latest improvements and fixes.
